what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG icon showing an upward-trending line ending in an arrow.
observations: The SVG uses a 24 by 24 viewBox with no explicit width or height.
The graphic is stroke-only with fill set to none.
Stroke width is 1.5 with round line caps and round joins.
A single path draws the zigzag trend line and the arrow arms at the upper-right end.
standouts: Single compact path encodes both the rising line and arrow affordance.
The icon is designed to inherit color from its surrounding text or UI context.
conventions: Follows common outline-icon conventions: 24x24 grid, currentColor stroke, no fill, rounded stroke ends.
Uses inline SVG attributes rather than CSS classes or external styling.
Keeps the asset self-contained in one svg element and one path element.
notes: A small reusable outline icon for upward trend or growth indicators.
questions: Should this icon rely only on currentColor, or should any default color be supplied by the consuming system?
Is the 1.5 stroke width intended to match the surrounding icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a trend-up icon, and the SVG depicts an upward-trending arrow.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a simple scalable UI icon for trend, growth, or upward movement while adapting to parent color styling.
contribution: Provides a reusable vector glyph for upward movement or positive trend states with color inheritance and compact stroke-based geometry.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 stroke-only SVG trend-up icon drawn with one path, currentColor stroke, no fill, and rounded line styling.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing upward trend or growth in a reusable design-system icon asset.
